name: N++ (NPLUSPLUS)
description: N++ is a fast-paced, momentum-based platformer about darting around obstacles, narrowly evading enemies and collecting gold in a beautiful minimalist landscape.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS *: 32-bit Windows Vista
  • Processor: Core i3 / AMD A6 2.4Ghz
  • Memory: 2 GB RAM
  • Graphics: 512MB VRAM NVIDIA GeForce 8xxx series / ATI Radeon HD 3xxx series. OpenGL 3.3 support
  • Storage: 650 MB available space
  • Additional Notes: Integrated Intel graphics are not supported. Intel HD 4000-series or better should work, but may have issues. The game may run on Windows XP, but we have not been able to test it.
comment1: Add this game to ur daily Wordle + Connections rotation and NEVER fall back on that decision
comment2:
A highly polished pure momentum platformer with a plethora of content. I also like the freedom of choosing between doing the levels by row for a gradual difficulty curve, or by column to get to the harder levels faster and for a better flow.

Just know that "pure" is VERY important here. There is no story, the graphics are minimal, and the soundtrack is basically just a recommended playlist of industrial/techno tracks. N++ is all about navigating obstacle courses. If you need more "context" around the gameplay, you may not like this game.

My main critique is how all-gold routes are structured. Getting all of the gold in a level can be an insanely hard challenge, and it's recommended to return to levels later so that you are better prepared. The problem is that a lot of these levels are specifically built around the all-gold route, and beating the level normally feels like you are basically skipping it. Oftentimes like a half or more of the screen is irrelevant if you aren't collecting all of the gold. This is more common in early levels, since they're sorted by normal route difficulty instead of all-gold route difficulty.
comment3:
This game [i] is [/i] pure flow state.

It's a perfect masterpiece that demands perfection from the player. Nothing can be added or taken away from this game to make it more perfect. Everything is earned through your skill, from levels to customization options, and there's literally thousands of levels on top of secret levels on top of secret challenges that unlock even more secret levels. As you play you'll literally delve deeper into the game and experience things that only an increasingly small fraction of the player base has experienced.

You're allowed to tackle any challenge that you want as you see fit. Stuck on a level? Just try one of the  other episodes available. Try getting all the gold or try a different challenge. You can do whatever you want and there's no down time as the game gradually unlocks based on what you do. Things that seem impossible gradually become manageable as you see your skill improve in real time. Some of the hardest things I've ever done in gaming have been in this game.

Overall it's a masterpiece. 10/10 would recommend.
